elements.sent-2411-001: The eccentricity matrix of a simple connected graph is derived from its distance matrix by preserving the largest non-zero distance in each row and column, while the other entries are set to zero.
elements.sent-2411-002: This article examines the $\epsilon$-spectrum, $\epsilon$-energy, $\epsilon$-inertia and irreducibility of the central graph (respectively complement of the central graph) of a triangle-free regular graph(respectively regular graph).
elements.sent-2411-003: Also look into the $\epsilon-$spectrum and the irreducibility of different central graph operations, such as central vertex join, central edge join, and central vertex-edge join.
elements.sent-2411-004: We also examine the $\epsilon-$ energy of some specific graphs.
elements.sent-2411-005: These findings allow us to construct new families of $\epsilon$-cospectral graphs and non $\epsilon$-cospectral $\epsilon-$equienergetic graphs.
elements.sent-2411-006: Additionally, we investigate certain upper and lower bounds for the eccentricity Wiener index of graphs.
elements.sent-2411-007: Also, provide an upper bound for the eccentricity energy of a self-centered graph.
elements.sent-2411-008: Keywords: eccentricity matrix, $\epsilon-$spectrum, $\epsilon-$ energy, central graph, irreducibility, $\epsilon-$Wiener index.
elements.sent-2411-010: All graphs considered in this paper are undirected, finite, and simple.
elements.sent-2411-011: Let $G=(V(G),E(G))$ be a $(p,q)$ graph with vertex set $V(G)=\{v_{1 ,\ldots , v_{p}\}$ and edge set $E(G)=\{e_{1 ,\ldots ,e_{q}\}.$ The complement $\overline{G}$ of $G$ is a graph with the vertex set $V(G)$ and edges consisting of pairs of nonadjacent vertices in $G.$ The line graph $L(G)$ of a graph $G$ is a graph whose vertex set is equal to the edge set of $G$, and two vertices in L(G) are adjacent if the corresponding edges in G share a common endpoint.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: The line graph of $L(G)$ is denoted as $L^{2 (G).$ The adjacency matrix $A(G)=(a_{i,j )$ of $G$ is a $p\times p$ matrix whose $(ij)^{th}$ entry $a_{i,j}$ is $1$ if the $i^{th}$ vertex is adjacent with the $j^{th}$ vertex $(i.e, v_{i}\sim v_{j )$ of $G$ and $0$ otherwise.}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: The eigenvalues of $A(G)$ are called $A-$eigenvalues of $G.$ The collection of all $A-$eigenvalues of $G$ together with their multiplicities are called $A-$spectra.
elements.sent-2411-014: The incidence matrix $R(G)=(r_{i,j )$ of $G$ is a $p\times q$ matrix whose $(ij)^{th}$ entry $r_{ij}$ is $1$ if the $i^{th}$ vertex of $G$ is incident with the $j^{th}$ edge of $G$ and $0$ otherwise.}
elements.sent-2411-015: The distance matrix $D(G)=(d_{i,j )$ of a graph $G$ is a $p\times p$ matrix whose $(ij)^{th}$ entry $d_{i,j}$ represents the distance between the vertices $v_{i}$ and $v_{j .$ The distance between the vertices $v_{i}$ and $v_{j}$ is denoted by $d(v_{i ,v_{j ).$ The degree of a vertex, $deg(v_{i ),$ is the number of edges incident with vertex $v_{i .$ The eccentricity of a vertex $v_{i}$ is defined as $e(v_{i )=\max \{d(v_{i ,v_{j ):v_{j}\in V(G)\}.$ The vertex $v_{k}$ is an eccentric vertex of $v_{i}$ if $d(v_{i , v_{k ) = e(v_{i ).$ The minimum and maximum eccentricities of all vertices of $G$ are called radius($r(G)$) and diameter$(diam(G))$ respectively.}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: A graph $G$ is self-centered if $r(G)$ and $diam(G)$ are the same.
elements.sent-2411-017: The total eccentricity of a graph is defined as $\varepsilon^{* (G)=\sum_{i=1 ^{p}\epsilon(v_{i ),$ and the eccentric connectivity index of $G$ is, $\zeta(G)=\sum_{i=1 ^{p deg(v_{i )e(v_{i ).$ The eccentricity matrix, $\epsilon(G)$ of a graph $G$ is a $p\times p$ matrix its entries are defined as \begin{equation}(\epsilon(G))_{ij}=\begin{cases} d(v_{i},v_{j}) \text{ if } d(v_{i},v_{j})=\min\{e(v_{i}),e(v_{j})\}, 0 \text{ otherwise.} \end{cases}\end{equation} A graph $G$ is $\epsilon-$regular if $\epsilon(i)=k,$ for all $i,$ where $\epsilon(i)=\sum_{j=1 ^{n}\epsilon(G))_{ij .$ Note that $\epsilon(G)$ is a real symmetric matrix, its eigenvalues are represented as $\epsilon-$eigenvalues of $G.$ If $\epsilon_{1}\geq \epsilon_{2 \geq , \ldots , \geq\epsilon_{k}$ are the distinct eigenvalues of $\epsilon(G)$ then $\epsilon-$spectrum of $\epsilon(G)$ is defined as \begin{equation}spec_{\epsilon}(G)= \epsilon_{1} \epsilon_{2} \cdots \epsilon_{k} m_{1} m_{2} \cdots m_{k} ,\end{equation} where $m_{i}$ denotes the algebraic multiplicity of the eigenvalue $\epsilon_{i .$ The eccentric graph of $G$, $G^{e}$ has the same vertex set as of $G$.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: In $ G^{e}$, two vertices $ v_{i $ and $ v_{j $ are adjacent if and only if $ d(v_{i , v_{j ) = \min\{e(v_{i ), e(v_{j )\}.$ Let $J$ and $I$ represent the all-one matrix and the identity matrix of appropriate order respectively and Q represents the equitable quotient matrix\cite{brouwer2011spectra .}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: If a permutation matrix $P$ exists such that \begin{equation}M=P^{T} N_{11} N_{12} 0 N_{22} P,\end{equation} where $N_{11}$ and $N_{22}$ are square block matrices, then $M$ is reducible.
elements.sent-2411-021: Unlike adjacency and distance matrices, the eccentricity matrix is not always irreducible.
elements.sent-2411-022: Some classes of graphs with irreducible or reducible eccentricity matrices are discussed in \cite{li2022inertia, MR4792389, MR4305906,MR3906706,MR4092627 The notion of eccentricity matrix was introduced and studied in \cite{MR3906706 .}}
elements.sent-2411-023: The eccentricity matrix is also known as $D_{MAX}$ matrix in the literature\cite{MR3136762 .}
elements.sent-2411-024: The $\epsilon-$spectral radius, $\rho_{\epsilon (G)$ of $G$ is the largest value among the absolute values of $\epsilon-$eigenvalues and $\epsilon-$energy, $E_{\epsilon (G)$ of $G$ is the sum of the absolute values of the $\epsilon-$eigenvalues.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: Two non-isomorphic graphs with the same order and the same $\epsilon-$spectrum are called $\epsilon-$cospectral graphs.
elements.sent-2411-027: Two graphs are $\epsilon-$equienergetic if they have the same $\epsilon-$ energy.
elements.sent-2411-029: But the converse need not be true.
elements.sent-2411-030: Construction of non $\epsilon-$ cospectral $\epsilon-$equienergetic graphs is an interesting problem in spectral graph theory.
elements.sent-2411-031: In this article, we explore some families of non $\epsilon-$cospectral $\epsilon-$equienergetic graphs.
elements.sent-2411-032: The eccentricity Wiener index ($\epsilon-$ Wiener index) of a graph $G$ is defined as, $W_{\epsilon (G)=\frac{1 {2}\sum_{ij (\epsilon(G))_{ij .$ Let $M$ be a symmetric matrix then the inertia of $M,$ $In(M)=(n_{+ (M), n_{- (M), n_{0 (M))$ which represents the number of positive, negative, and zero eigenvalues of $M$, respectively.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: In literature, the inertia of eccentricity matrices of a few graphs are available, see \cite{MR4730406,li2022inertia, MR4110109, MR4446122, MR4305906 .}
elements.sent-2411-034: In this article, we discuss about the eccentricity inertia of some graphs.
elements.sent-2411-035: In graph theory, operations on graphs play an important role in constructing graphs from given graphs.
elements.sent-2411-036: See \cite{MR4792389, MR4305906 for important studies on eccentricity spectra of graph operations.}
elements.sent-2411-037: The central graph, $C[G],$ of a graph $G$ is a unary graph operation that is obtained from $G$ by adding new vertices, $I(G)$, to each edge of $G$ and joining all of its non-adjacent vertices.
elements.sent-2411-038: In \cite{MR4657005, MR4780061, MR4150216 , the authors introduced various graph operations on central graphs and determined spectrum (adjacency, distance).}
elements.sent-2411-039: Motivated by these findings, we examine the $\epsilon$-spectrum of central graphs, central vertex joins, central edge joins, and central vertex-edge joins of graphs.
elements.sent-2411-040: In \cite{MR4574900, MR4727625 there are some applications of the $\epsilon-$Wiener index as a lower bound for the eccentricity spectral radius and eccentricity spectral spread.}
elements.sent-2411-041: Inspired by these studies, we establish the relationship between the eccentricity Wiener index and the total eccentricity of a graph.
elements.sent-2411-042: This article is organized as follows: In Section 2, we gather some preliminary results.
elements.sent-2411-043: In Section 3, we obtain the $\epsilon-$spectrum, irreducibility, $\epsilon-$spectral radius, $\epsilon-$energy, and the inertia of the central graph of a graph.
elements.sent-2411-044: Furthermore, the $\epsilon-$spectrum and $\epsilon-$energy of the complement of the central graph are estimated.
elements.sent-2411-045: Moreover, the $\epsilon-$ spectrum, irreducibility, $\epsilon-$ Wiener index, and a lower bound for $\epsilon-$spectral radius are found for the central vertex join and central edge join of graphs, where the first graph is a regular triangle-free and the second graph is a regular graph.
elements.sent-2411-046: In addition, the $\epsilon-$spectrum of the central vertex-edge join operation of a triangle-free regular graph with two regular graphs is computed.
elements.sent-2411-047: Using these graph operations, new families of $\epsilon-$ cospectral graphs and non-$\epsilon$-cospectral $\epsilon$-equienergetic graphs are constructed.
elements.sent-2411-048: Additionally, we obtain the $\epsilon-$ energy of some particular class of graphs.
elements.sent-2411-049: Section 4 provides some lower and upper bounds for the $\epsilon-$ Wiener index of $G,$ also we provide a Nordhus-Guddum type upper bound for the $\epsilon-$ Wiener index.
elements.sent-2411-050: Moreover, we present an upper bound for the $\epsilon-$Wiener index for trees and for the $\epsilon-$ energy of the self-centered graph.
elements.sent-2411-051: This section contains some definitions and results that will be used in the next sections.
elements.sent-2411-052: The central vertex join of $G_{1}$ and $G_{2}$ is the graph $C[G_{1 ]\dot{\vee G_{2}$ is obtained by $C[G_{1 ]$ and $G_{2}$ by joining each vertex of $G_{1}$ with every vertex of $G_{2 .$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-053: The central edge join of $G_{1}$ and $G_{2}$ is the graph $C[G_{1 ]\veebar{G_{2 }$ is obtained by $C[G_{1 ]$ and $G_{2}$ by joining each vertex corresponding to the edges of $G_{1}$ ($ I(G)$) with every vertex of $G_{2 .$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-054: The central vertex-edge join of $G_{1}$ with $G_{2}$ and $G_{3}$ is the graph $C[G_{1 ] \vee (G_{2 ^{V \cup G_{3 ^{E ),$ is obtained from $C[G_{1 ],$ $G_{2}$ and $G_{3}$ by joining each vertex of $G_{1}$ with every vertex of $G_{2}$ and each vertex corresponding to the edges of $G_{1}$ with every vertex of $G_{3 .$.}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-056: Then $RR^{T =A+rI$, $R^{T R=B+2I$, where $B$ is the adjacency matrix of $L(G)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-057: Also, if $J$ is an all-one matrix of appropriate order, then $JR=2J=R^{T J$ and $JR^{T =rJ=RJ.$.}}
elements.sent-2411-058: Also, $Z$ is an eigenvector corresponding to the eigenvalue $-2$ if and only if $RZ=0$.
elements.sent-2411-059: Then the matrix $\epsilon(G)$ is irreducible if and only if $G^{e}$ is a connected graph.
elements.sent-2411-060: Then $diam(L(G))=1$ if and only if $G=K_{3}$ or $G=K_{1,p-1 .$.}

elements.section-2411-003: Eccentricity spectrum of central graph operations of graphs
elements.sent-2411-063: This section explores the $\epsilon-$ spectral properties of the central graph, as well as the complement of the central graph of a graph.
elements.sent-2411-064: As an application, we construct new families of $\epsilon-$ cospectral and non-$\epsilon-$ cospectral $\epsilon-$equienergetic graphs.

elements.sent-2411-080: Estimating the eccentricity spectrum of $C[G]$ for a general graph is a complex process.
elements.sent-2411-081: However, using the following result, ascertaining the eccentricity spectrum of $\overline{C[G]}$ is quite easy.

elements.section-2411-004: Some bounds for eccentricity Wiener index of graphs
elements.sent-2411-157: This section is devoted to determining some lower and upper bounds of the $\epsilon-$ Wiener index of graphs.

Therefore, \begin{equation}\label{monotone} \epsilon_{1}+\sqrt{(p-1)(||\epsilon(G)||_{2}^{2}-\epsilon_{1}^{2})}\leq \frac{2W_{\epsilon}(G)}{p}+\sqrt{(p-1)\biggl(||\epsilon(G)||_{2}^{2}-\frac{4W_{\epsilon}(G)^{2}}{p^{2}}\biggr)}.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-192: The graph operations such as central graph of a graph, central vertex join, central edge join and central vertex-edge join have been used to expand the collection of existing graphs for which $\epsilon-$ spectrum, irreducibility, $\epsilon-$ inertia, and $\epsilon-$ energy are known.
elements.sent-2411-193: Also, the $\epsilon-$ energy of some classes of graphs are estimated.
elements.sent-2411-194: These results allow us to construct new families of $\epsilon-$ cospectral graphs and non $\epsilon-$cospectral $\epsilon-$equienergetic graphs.
elements.sent-2411-195: Additionally, lower and upper bounds for the $\epsilon-$ Wiener index are determined by using the eccentricity connectivity index and the total eccentricity of a graph.
elements.sent-2411-196: Moreover, an upper bound for the $\epsilon-$energy of self-centered graphs is provided.. \bibliographystyle{plain.}
